purpose: Machine-readable documentation of the script sequence that rebuilds the project's derived CSV files and final strict student-visa SQLite database from preserved raw sources.
runtime_requirements: Python standard library only for the documented build scripts
Official UN M49 source at data/UNSD — Methodology.csv

primary_command.description: Runs the complete local raw-source extraction, transformation, strict database load, and validation chain.

optional_parameters.effect: Reuses existing source-level CSV files and skips extract_csv.py and extract_us.py.
Skips validate_database.py after the database load.

primary_command.execution_behavior: Stops immediately if any child script exits unsuccessfully.

script_chain.description: Extracts source-level CSV files from the preserved New Zealand HTML, Australian XLSX pivot caches, United Kingdom XLSX sheets, and Canada HTML, CSV, and XLSX sources.

result.australia_local_filename_caveat: The preserved raw and extracted filenames contain 2026-02-28, but the official metadata and pivot-cache period identify the downloaded resources as 2026-04-30.

script_chain.description: Extracts United States F1 and M1 primary-student issuance counts. F2 and M2 dependent classes are excluded.
Combines Canada source-level CSV files into analysis-ready country-of-residence outcomes, intake and issuance measures, and citizenship-based permit volumes.
Extracts the OLLO Q-2700 French-speaking or bilingual study-permit subset and writes its SQLite-only common-schema file without modifying SQLite during the full rebuild.

optional_parameters.effect: Replaces Q-2700 rows in an existing database. Not used because load_sqlite.py includes the subset automatically.

script_chain.description: Transforms supported source-level and processed inputs into the common long schema using the shared country normalizer, excluding explicitly identifiable dependant and mixed applicant cohorts.
Builds the strict student-only SQLite artifact atomically, filters incompatible common-table categories, includes the Canada Q-2700 SQLite-only subset, creates indexes, and runs ANALYZE.

strict_filter.Australia: visa_category = student visa and applicant_type = Primary
strict_filter.United Kingdom: visa_category = Study and applicant_type is Main Applicant or main_applicant_only
strict_filter.United States: F1 and M1 rows with applicant_type = primary; F2 and M2 are excluded

script_chain.description: Validates reference integrity, aliases, Australian source integrity, common/SQLite normalization consistency, measure constraints, and database provenance, then writes the unmatched-label audit.
checks: The official UN M49 source has the required schema, 248 unique country/area names, and valid three-digit M49 codes.
Australian count measures are non-negative integers.
Australian grant totals plus refusal totals equal decision totals.
Australian Higher Education and Primary records are unique when all pivot-cache dimensions are retained.
Country normalization is consistent across the common CSV, SQLite-only subset, and SQLite database.
No exact official UNSD country or area name remains unmatched.
Aggregates, unknown labels, and unmatched labels have no canonical UN M49 country assigned.
Count measures are non-negative integers and percentage measures are within 0 through 100.
Every SQLite row has source-file metadata.
No explicit Secondary, Dependant, dependent, All-applicant aggregate, F2, or M2 row remains in generated common artifacts or SQLite.

primary.description: Strict student and study visa query database, excluding explicitly identifiable dependant and mixed-applicant cohorts and including the explicitly identified Canada Q-2700 language subset.

common_csv.scope_note: Excludes explicitly identifiable dependant and mixed-applicant cohorts. Retains broader source categories that remain distinguishable through visa and coverage fields.

normalization_audit.review_note: Remaining unmatched labels are absent from the supplied current UNSD list, chiefly Taiwan, Kosovo, historical countries or areas, and subnational areas.

separate_maintenance_script.description: Refreshes normalization fields in an existing database with the same shared normalizer. It is not part of a full rebuild because load_sqlite.py writes current normalization directly.
